name: vMix Caller
description: Manage Zoom and Teams callers in vMix from the SignalScope hub dashboard. Hub/client architecture: the operator works on the hub; the site node alongside vMix polls for commands and executes them locally — works through NAT with no direct hub-to-site connectivity needed. Hub shows a site selector, vMix IP/port config (pushed to client node on save), meeting controls (join/leave/mute/camera/mute-all), and a live participants list reported from vMix. One-click Put On Air sends ZoomSelectParticipantByName. Optional caller video preview via SRT-to-HLS bridge — LAN bridge (vMix pushes SRT to local machine, browser accesses directly) or hub-server bridge (proxied securely through SignalScope). Presenter view at /hub/vmixcaller/presenter — bookmark-friendly page for the studio email machine: one-click join from saved meetings list, full-screen video feed, in-call mute/camera/leave toolbar. Keyboard shortcuts: M = mute self, C = toggle camera.

name: Brand Screen
description: Animated full-screen studio branding display. Studios (physical screens) are assigned Stations (brand configs) — change the assignment instantly via REST API and all browsers update in real time over SSE. Per-station logo upload, brand colour, background style (particles, aurora, waves, beams, grid, burst, haze, minimal), and logo animation (orbit rings, pulse, glow, float, spin, glitch, bounce). Background hue derived from brand colour — the entire screen lives in your brand palette. Logo fills most of the screen. Assign a live audio stream to animate orbit rings, spin speed, bounce kicks, pulse tempo, bloom, beams, burst rays, grid lines, and particles with real audio levels at 150 ms. Broadcast clock, On Air badge, live now-playing from Zetta or any JSON API, message-from-hub amber banner. Token-auth kiosk URL per studio. Yodeck compatible. Anti-burn-in pixel drift. Hub-only.

name: 25/7 PDM
description: 25/7 Programme Delay Manager monitor. Installed on client nodes — connects to the PDM unit via TCP, polls delay depth at 1 Hz, and pushes live data to the hub every 2 s. Hub displays a pill per reporting site showing delay in ms and frame equivalents at 25/30 fps. Configurable IP address and port.

name: FM Scanner
description: Live FM radio receiver with RDS display, frequency history, presets, and band scan. Streams audio from a remote site's RTL-SDR dongle (role=scanner) to the browser in real time.

name: Web SDR
description: Browser-based software defined radio with scrolling waterfall display. Tune any frequency within the capture bandwidth. WFM, NFM, and AM demodulation modes. Requires an RTL-SDR dongle on the client machine.

name: DAB Scanner
description: DAB digital radio browser. Scans all Band III channels to discover services and streams selected services as MP3 audio. Displays DLS scrolling text. Raspberry Pi supported. Requires welle-cli and ffmpeg on the client machine.

name: Logger
description: Compliance logger for continuous 24/7 recording of all monitored streams. Hub mode aggregates recordings from all connected sites — browse timelines, play audio, and export segments with mark in/out. Supports now-playing metadata from Planet Radio, Triton, or any JSON API. Requires ffmpeg.

name: Meter Wall
description: Full-screen audio meter wall showing live PPM-style level bars for every monitored stream across all connected sites. Peak hold, LUFS readout, alert flash, RDS/DLS now-playing, site grouping, and kiosk mode. Stereo L/R bars at 150 ms update rate.

name: Livewire
description: Axia Livewire source discovery. Listens for LWAP advertisements on 239.192.255.3:4001 and builds a live inventory of sources across all connected sites — Node, Stream ID, friendly name, multicast address, and online/stale status. Client nodes push their source tables to the hub every 30 s. Sources grouped by node with accordion display, sortable columns (Stream ID, Last Seen, Status), and live search. Stale-source alerts via email, webhook, and push with per-channel configuration. Create Input buttons on client nodes. Hub-only overview shows all sites.

name: Zetta Integration
description: RCS Zetta broadcast automation integration. Multi-instance SOAP monitor — shows live sequencer state per station: mode (Auto/Manual/Voice Track), status (PLAYING/QUEUED/NEXT), GAP, ETM, now-playing title/artist/cart/category, and upcoming queue. Detects commercial/spot blocks in real time. Per-station broadcast chain assignment with live status chips on the Broadcast Chains page. Includes a SOAP debug explorer. Requires zeep.

name: Morning Report
description: Daily morning briefing auto-generated at 06:00 covering the previous day. Plain-English fault summary, per-chain health table with on-air %, traffic-light trend pills, hourly fault heatmap, stream quality summary, and Zetta automation health section (failovers, mode changes, GAP warnings, faults during ad breaks). Hub-only.

name: Signal Path Latency
description: Signal path latency tracking for broadcast chains. Polls all connected sites for comparator delay measurements, stores 90 days of history, and displays per-comparator sparklines with stable/drifting/alert badges. Hub-only.

name: Codec Monitor
description: Real-time connection monitor for broadcast contribution codecs — Comrex ACCESS/BRIC-Link, Tieline Gateway/ViA, Prodys Quantum ST, and APT/WorldCast Quantum. Polls via HTTP scraping or SNMP and fires CODEC_FAULT/RECOVERY alerts. Runs on hub and client nodes.

name: Push Server
description: Centralised push notification server for iOS and Android. Holds APNs and FCM credentials and delivers notifications on behalf of any SignalScope installation that points its Push Server URL here. Hub-only.

name: Wallboard
description: TV-optimised broadcast wallboard with live chain status cards, station logos, PPM-style level meters, alert ticker, and full customisation. Upload logos per chain, select which streams to display, toggle LUFS/now-playing/hero banner. Gradient avatars, health bars, zone-coloured meters at 150 ms update rate. Zetta mode badges on chain cards, drag-to-reorder chain cards via settings drawer, anti-burn-in pixel drift. Hub-only.

name: PTP Clock
description: Full-screen GPS-accurate wall clock for broadcast studios. Digital and analog modes, live PTP sync status, offset and jitter display, custom branding via URL parameter.

name: Icecast Streaming
description: Manage Icecast2 streaming servers on client nodes. Re-streams any monitored input to Icecast2. Hub overview shows live listener counts and stream status from all connected sites. Requires ffmpeg; Icecast2 installed separately.

name: Listener
description: Live stream listener for presenters and producers. Station cards with live level meters and one-tap audio playback. Designed for non-technical users — auto-reconnects, animated equalizer, volume control, mobile-friendly. Hub-only.

name: Producer View
description: Simplified hub view for producers and presenters. Station status cards with live levels and plain-English chain fault history. Users assigned the Producer role go directly here on login. Hub-only.

name: AzuraCast
description: AzuraCast web radio integration. Live now-playing data, listener counts, and station health across all connected sites. Fires AZURACAST_FAULT/RECOVERY alerts and optionally cross-references with SignalScope silence detection.

name: IP Link
description: Browser-based IP codec and SIP softphone. WebRTC rooms with shareable talent URLs — low-latency Opus audio, bidirectional IFB, live level meters. Permanent rooms support server-side routing with Livewire/AES67 multicast output. Server-side SIP registration (hub holds multiple SIP accounts permanently — no browser needed). Requires aiortc, av, and numpy for server-side WebRTC.

name: Sync Capture
description: Multi-site synchronized audio capture. Select inputs from any connected sites, set a duration, and capture — the hub timestamps the command and each client grabs its rolling buffer at the same moment. All clips presented together with inline players, EBU R128 analysis, waveforms, and DAW session export (REAPER/Audition). Hub-only.

name: Studio Board
description: Large-format studio display for outside broadcast studios. Shows presenter/show info, now playing, live level meters, mic live status, chain health, and Zetta automation queue. Fully configurable admin page with Studios (physical screens) and Brands (station presets) — assign a brand to a studio in one REST call. REST API for mic live, brand assignment, and legacy chain assignment. Themeable (dark/Bauer/corp via ?theme=), clock header, big presenter countdown timer, message-from-hub banner, anti-burn-in pixel drift. Server-side presenter image cache. Hub-only.
